what-a-professional-audit-is: Most "audits" are scans. A real audit is someone reading your site.
five-issues-on-every-site: The five issues that show up on almost every site, and how to fix each one.
price-gap-explained: Not all audits are the same. The gap between $0 and $350 and $3,000 is not arbitrary.
pagespeed-90-doesnt-mean-good: High PageSpeed score with silent conversion failures is the most common trap we see.
what-it-feels-like-to-receive-an-audit: A single document you can read, understand, and act on without hiring anyone else to translate it.
three-tier-audit-market: The $350 audit is the tier the market was missing, and most owners we work with belong there.
false-confidence-problem: The most damaging audit is the one that tells you everything is working when things are silently broken.
what-automated-audits-get-wrong: Automated audits measure what they can measure. They miss what actually moves revenue.
more-traffic-isnt-the-goal: Audit and fix. Then drive traffic. In that order. Anything else is throwing money at a problem.
diy-builders-dont-audit-honestly: Built-in audits flag what the platform can upsell you on, not what is actually broken.
why-28-standard-assessments: The same twenty-eight checks, every site, every time. That consistency is what makes findings credible.
why-some-audits-include-more: Every audit starts at twenty-eight. Most include more. The "more" are there because your site needed them.
human-in-the-loop: We run the automation, then add judgment. You pay for the judgment. That is what makes it useful.
diagnosis-vs-remedy: Every critical and high-value finding gets step-by-step instructions. No caps, no shortcuts.
plain-english-over-jargon: Two layers: what it means in plain English on top, the technical detail underneath if you want it.
priority-ranked-remedies: Do the criticals this week. Do the high values next. Skip the nice-to-haves with a clear conscience.
built-to-implement-yourself: Every remedy is written so a non-developer can do it, step by step, without hiring anyone.
roi-math-of-one-fix: A broken contact form on iPhone costs you $4,800 a year. The audit is $350. One fix pays it back.
trust-signals-youre-missing: A visitor lands and has ten seconds to decide to call you. Are they seeing reason to trust?
conversion-path-failures: Most conversion breaks are silent. The visitor never tells you they left. The phone just rings less.
accessibility-issues-cost-customers: A customer who could not use your site never emails to say so. They buy from someone whose site worked.
schema-the-invisible-seo-boost: Half of all small business sites have no schema. ChatGPT cannot recommend you without it.
website-audit-research-2026: After auditing 24 sites, 96% had critical issues. Most owners had no idea. You are in good company.
